# Resonance Architecture Framework

This page presents the Resonance Architecture hypothesis: a structured proposal that ancient megalithic cultures may have systematically employed vibroacoustic phenomena—including vibration-assisted friction modulation, piezoelectric excitation of quartz-bearing stone, and tribological self-organisation—to achieve precision stoneworking capabilities. 

Authored by Justin Bogner, December 2025 / Revised April 2026.

## Structure
- The Mystery: Details 5 architectural anomalies (Transport, Petrie drill cores, Polygonal joints, Boss placement, Acoustic resonance).
- Physical Foundations: Explains friction modulation, piezoelectric excitation, ultrasonic abrasion, and tribological self-organisation.
- The Validation: Outlines predictions and falsification criteria for the hypothesis.
